No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not address health, well-being, or related medical/social services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) focused on developing essential English language and communication skills for employment and career preparedness (Target 4.4).
evidence: be well-equipped with essential English language skills in job hunting
career preparedness and essential business communication skills
be able to communicate effectively in a business meeting and negotiation

Gender Equality.reason: No specific course content or objectives address gender equality or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly focuses on 'career preparedness,' 'job hunting,' and 'job interview preparation,' directly supporting full and productive employment and decent work (Targets 8.5, 8.6).
evidence: be well-equipped with essential English language skills in job hunting

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course promotes innovation through 'video CV' production and develops skills for industry activities via the 'A&M simulation project' (Target 9.b).
evidence: produce a video CV to promote oneself on the job market
an A&M simulation project to negotiate an acquisition or merger proposal

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By equipping students with job-hunting skills, the course indirectly promotes individual economic inclusion (Target 10.2), though it doesn't address systemic inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B, 'understand... sustainability issues,' provides an indirect link to adopting sustainable business practices (Target 12.6), though it's not a primary focus.
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course lacks specific content or objectives directly related to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or sustainable land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The 'Negotiation in Business' module provides conflict resolution skills, and the 'ethics' core competency indirectly relates to transparent institutions (Target 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: Negotiation in Business : Language and Skills
B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The 'M&A Meeting Simulation' and 'teamwork' core competency foster collaboration skills, reflecting micro-level principles of partnerships (Target 17.17).
evidence: E. 團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams as well as develop leadership skills.)
